name: issue-427-natvent-sleep-floor-churn
description: Issue #427: overnight WHF nat-vent session with indoor drifting down through the sleep window while comfort_heat (daytime) and sleep_heat (overnight) diverge. Before the fix, Phase 2's proactive floor exit (check_natural_vent_conditions) read the flat daytime comfort_heat instead of the sleep-aware canonical floor (_nat_vent_reactivation_floor()) that every other exit/reactivation site already uses -- so during the sleep window it believed the floor was already breached (negative time_to_floor) hours before it actually was, tearing the whole nat-vent session down every ~5-15 min. The physical fan kept running independently (thermostat circulation / hardware lag), which the coordinator saw as 'running (untracked)' and re-adopted as a brand-new nat-vent session via reconcile_fan_on_startup(), which Phase 2 then immediately re-exited on the next tick -- a self-inflicted exit/untracked/reconcile-adopt loop for 6 straight hours. After the fix, Phase 2 uses the same sleep-aware floor as Priority-1/reconcile, so the session survives continuously through the sleep window and the fan only cycles via the correct in-session thermostatic mechanism (nat_vent_temperature_check), never tearing down session identity.
notes: Occupant impact: the resident had a bedroom window open overnight for free cooling. Before the fix, the activity log/notifications showed 5+ churning entries every 5-15 minutes for 6 hours (fan activated -> proactive exit -> fan running untracked -> fan re-adopted -> exit again), which is both confusing noise in the daily briefing and (combined with daytime activity) a major contributor to exceeding the activity log's entry cap. The physical fan behavior was arguably fine throughout -- what was broken was the system's own bookkeeping repeatedly destroying and recreating session identity it should never have torn down, given conditions never meaningfully changed.

Config: fan_mode=whole_house_fan; comfort_heat=68F (daytime floor) vs sleep_heat=64F (overnight floor) -- deliberately several degrees apart, matching the divergence that caused the bug. sleep_time=22:00, wake_time=06:30 so the whole overnight timeline (00:00-06:00) falls inside the sleep window. thermal_model.k_passive=-0.08 (slow, matches the real report's multi-hour gradual drift) with confidence=high so Phase 2's ODE-projected time_to_floor is active throughout.

Three-exercise protocol (CLAUDE.md Sec.10): (a) Scenario effectiveness: reverting the fix (restoring the flat `self.config.get("comfort_heat", 70)` read and removing the `>= 0` guard in Phase 2) causes the 02:00 and 04:00 assertions to fail -- production_outcome_at would return "nat_vent_predicted_floor_exit" instead of "natural_ventilation", and the final nat_vent_still_active assertion would fail since the session would have been torn down. (b) Docs<->production alignment: docs/08-COMPUTATION-REFERENCE.md nat-vent section must document that Phase 2 uses the same _nat_vent_reactivation_floor() as Priority-1/reconcile before this is promoted to golden. (c) User outcome if this regresses: the occupant again sees hours of churning fan-activated/exit/untracked/reconcile notifications for a session that never needed to end, eroding trust in the activity log and daily briefing (CLAUDE.md 'Daily Briefing as Primary UI').

This harness (tools/sim_harness) drives the real AutomationEngine only -- it does not model the coordinator-level 'running (untracked)' detection or reconcile_fan_on_startup() re-adoption (those live in coordinator.py and are triggered by the coordinator's periodic backstop, not by AutomationEngine methods the harness dispatches). This scenario proves the AutomationEngine-level root cause (Phase 2 tearing down the session) is fixed; it does not directly exercise the coordinator-level symptom chain. That chain is documented as a consequence of this root cause in the issue, not as a separately-tested code path.
Promote to golden/ once reviewed and confirmed by the user per the Golden Simulation Test Policy.

events.note: Evening classification: mild day, hvac_mode=off. Comfort band armed [68/74].
Evening: indoor 70F, outdoor 60F -- outdoor cooler than indoor, above both floors.
Window opens. outdoor 60F < indoor 70F, indoor > comfort_heat 68F, outdoor <= threshold 77F -- nat-vent activates, WHF turns on.

Sleep window active (22:00-06:30). indoor=67F: below daytime comfort_heat(68F) but above sleep_heat(64F). passive_rate = -0.08*(67-55) = -0.96F/hr. OLD buggy floor (flat 68): time_to_floor = (67-68)/0.96 = -1.04h -> negative, old code wrongly exits. NEW canonical sleep-aware floor (64): time_to_floor = (67-64)/0.96 = 3.13h -- comfortably above MIN_VIABLE_NAT_VENT_HOURS(1.0h) -- session correctly stays active.

indoor continues drifting down to 66F. passive_rate = -0.08*(66-54) = -0.96F/hr. OLD floor(68): time_to_floor = (66-68)/0.96 = -2.08h (matches the real #427 report's '-2.09 hr' churn readings). NEW floor(64): time_to_floor = (66-64)/0.96 = 2.08h -- still comfortably above threshold, session stays active with no exit.

indoor at 65F, closest approach to sleep_heat(64F) this scenario reaches. passive_rate = -0.08*(65-53) = -0.96F/hr. NEW floor(64): time_to_floor = (65-64)/0.96 = 1.04h -- just above the 1.0h threshold, session still correctly stays active (proves the fix holds even near its own margin, not just far from the floor).
Still inside the sleep window (before 06:30 wake_time). Same reading as 04:00 -- confirms the session has remained continuously active across multiple hours/ticks, not just survived a single check.

assertions.reason: Sensor opens with outdoor 60F < indoor 70F, outdoor <= threshold 77F -- nat-vent activates, WHF turns on.
Sleep-aware floor (64F) puts the predicted floor 3.13h away -- Phase 2 must not fire, so the last recorded decision is still the 22:05 activation, not a floor-exit.
Same as above at the point matching the real report's -2.09h old-code reading -- with the sleep-aware floor, time_to_floor is +2.08h and Phase 2 does not fire.
Even at the closest approach to the sleep floor in this scenario (time_to_floor=1.04h, just above the 1.0h threshold), Phase 2 correctly does not fire.
After 8 hours and 4 re-evaluations spanning the whole sleep window, the nat-vent session must still be the SAME continuous session -- no proactive-exit/reconcile-adopt churn occurred at any point.

verdict.summary: During the sleep window, Phase 2's proactive floor exit correctly uses the sleep-aware canonical floor (matching Priority-1 and reconcile) instead of the flat daytime comfort_heat, so a nat-vent session with indoor hovering between the two floors survives continuously for hours instead of being torn down and re-adopted every 5-15 minutes.
verdict.observed_behavior: natural_ventilation at 22:05 -> session persists unchanged through 00:00, 02:00, 04:00, 06:00 despite indoor drifting from 67F down to 65F (approaching but never below the 64F sleep floor) -- no nat_vent_predicted_floor_exit events, no reconcile-adopt churn, _natural_vent_active remains True at the end.
verdict.expected_behavior: Phase 2 (check_natural_vent_conditions' proactive floor exit) reads _nat_vent_reactivation_floor() -- the same sleep-aware floor Priority-1 and reconcile_fan_on_startup already use -- and only exits when time_to_floor is genuinely non-negative and below MIN_VIABLE_NAT_VENT_HOURS. A negative time_to_floor (floor already at/below indoor) is left to the Priority-1 hard exit or nat_vent_temperature_check's in-session cycling, never destroying session identity for a condition that hasn't actually changed.
